title: Process: migrate from dependabot to renovatebot?
body:
As all of you know who have a repo fork of podman, dependabot wastes  your time every day with nasty bump attempts. It's been a known bug for years, with no action or fixes, but over the weekend someone [commented](https://github.com/dependabot/dependabot-core/issues/2804#issuecomment-1220769377):
>I can't really recommend dependabot to my colleagues or clients anymore. Apparently M$, the owners of GitHub, use [renovatebot](https://github.com/renovatebot/renovate) over dependabot. Lots of these issues are already fixed in renovatebot. Like being able to [disable a fork](https://docs.renovatebot.com/configuration-options/#includeforks). Or being able to control the [PR labels](https://docs.renovatebot.com/configuration-options/#addlabels), something https://github.com/dependabot/dependabot-core/issues/3465.

Whoever manages our dependabot setup: could you please look into this, and migrate if that's the right thing to do?

comments.body: Curious, I never ran into this issue in a fork.
I can confirm that dependabot does occasionally open PRs against my fork of Podman
It runs on all of my forks.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Oh sure I can look into it, thanks for pinging me.

Holy-smokes renovate has a lot of options.  I've added it + basic configuration to the c/automation branch to give it a spin.  So far so good, but I have one main concern:

It (like dependabot) wants to create upstream branches for it's PRs.  However, in the past this has resulted in some goofiness with our CI system (i.e. it doesn't run or won't run).  There also tend to be a more limited/restricted set of people who can force-push directly to upstream branches.  I've also not yet found if/how it's possible (or needed) to limit renovate's actions to only `main` and ignore all the release-branches we maintain.

Update:  I (finally) was able to get Renovate configured and working pretty well on the containers/image repository.  It's enabled along-side of Dependabot so we can spend some time drag-racing (comparing update PRs).  Once things stabilize on that repo, we can decide about moving forward with others.
Started to migrate containers/skopeo to Renovate, encountered an "interesting" issue WRT containers/common version retraction.

containers/skopeo has been onboarded.  I'm going to close this issue as "complete", though I will continue the migration effort independently.  Time and experience may prove Renovate isn't the appropriate tool for all repos, and/or the current tire-kicking trials may reveal "abandon-ship" level problems.  So in all those cases there's no reason to keep this issue open.
